# Citrus & Salt · Tequila & Mezcal Bar in Downtown Bryan, TX

Last updated: 2026-05-03

> Citrus and Salt is a craft tequila and mezcal bar on the ground floor at 121 N Main St in Historic Downtown Bryan, Texas. The only all-agave cocktail bar in the Bryan and College Station area. Owned by Dustin Batson, a Marine Corps veteran voted Best Bartender in Texas. Open Tuesday through Saturday, 7 pm to 2 am. Walk-ins only. Five miles and about ten minutes from Kyle Field and the Texas A&M campus, with no stadium traffic to fight before or after games.

## What it is

Every drink starts with tequila or mezcal. Over one hundred agave spirits on the back bar, from blanco to extra añejo, from espadín to tobalá. Craft cocktails, frozen margaritas, agave flights, and the full Mexican beer wall. The room is a cocktail bar, not a nightclub. The DJs are upstairs at Vice, Thursday through Saturday starting at 10 pm.

## Address and contact

- 121 N Main St, Historic Downtown Bryan, TX 77803, ground floor.
- Phone: (832) 285-4598.
- Email: citrusandsaltbryan@gmail.com.
- Website: https://citrusandsaltbryan.com.

## Hours

- Tuesday through Saturday, 7 pm to 2 am.
- Closed Sunday and Monday.
- Open every Aggie home football Saturday. Walk-ins only.

## Reservations

Walk-ins only. The bar does not take reservations for the regular service. Private events and full buyouts are bookable by phone or email: bachelor and bachelorette parties, birthdays, Ring Day group dinners, rehearsal dinners, and corporate happy hours.

## Owner

Dustin Batson. Marine Corps veteran. Voted Best Bartender in Texas. Bar Boss National Competition Finalist (2025). Same owner as Word of Mouth Speakeasy, Concrete Rodeo, West End Elixir Company, and Vice Downtown Bryan upstairs. Education and background: Texas hospitality, multi-decade craft cocktail focus.

## What is on the bar

- Over 100 tequilas and mezcals, list rotates. Producers represented include Fortaleza, Siete Leguas, Siembra Valles, G4, Tapatío, Tequila Ocho, Don Fulano, El Tesoro, Tears of Llorona, Clase Azul, Mijenta, Lalo, ArteNOM, Don Julio, and Patrón. Mezcal producers include Del Maguey, Vago, Rey Campero, Mezcal Unión, Bozal, and Ilegal.
- Craft cocktails. Three confirmed signatures: The Diablo del Playa (reposado, orange curaçao, habanero syrup, pineapple, lime), The Slow Burn (blanco, triple sec, cucumber and jalapeño syrup, lime), and Soot, Spice and Everything Nice (mezcal, orange curaçao, cinnamon syrup, orange juice).
- Frozen margaritas, classic lime, fresh citrus, real agave.
- The full Mexican beer wall.

## Why downtown Bryan

A third of Bryan is Mexican or Mexican-American. Four generations of families with strong opinions about agave. Downtown Bryan has served tequila since 1898, when Manuel Rodriguez opened the first Mexican restaurant in town a few blocks from the present location. Citrus and Salt was built intentionally for the people in Bryan and College Station who already know what good agave tastes like. Five miles from Kyle Field and the Texas A&M campus.

## Parking

Free street parking on N Main and surrounding blocks after 6 pm. City lot one block east. No valet.

## Dress code

Come as you are. It is a cocktail bar, not a nightclub.

## Difference between tequila and mezcal

Tequila is made only from blue Weber agave, almost all of it from Jalisco. Mezcal is broader, made from over thirty agave varieties (espadín, tobalá, madrecuixe, tepextate) across nine Mexican states, roasted in earthen pits. The smoke comes from the roast.

## Same building, different rooms

Vice Downtown Bryan, the hookah lounge and dance floor, lives on the floor above. Vice is open Tuesday through Saturday, 8 pm to 2 am. Same ownership, separate rooms. Citrus and Salt is the ground-floor cocktail bar; Vice is upstairs.

## Service area

Bryan, College Station, the Texas A&M campus, and the broader Brazos County area.

## Pages

- Home: https://citrusandsaltbryan.com/
- News and FAQ: https://citrusandsaltbryan.com/faq/
